summary: Repository-wide cleanup PR that ran clang-format over src. It touched 287 files across melee and sysdolphin code, with formatting-only edits such as line wrapping, brace placement, cast spacing, comment spacing, removal of excess blank lines, and reflowing long expressions. No PR body, review comments, or explicit rationale were present in the slice, so the value is inferred from the title and diff: normalize style across source files to reduce manual formatting noise in future decomp work.
what_changed: Ran clang-format over src, affecting 287 changed files with 2198 additions and 1719 deletions according to the PR slice.
Reflowed long arithmetic, ternary, assignment, and function-call expressions, especially visible in src/melee/cm/camera.c.
Normalized function definitions such as inline helpers by splitting long parameter lists and placing opening braces according to the configured style.
Expanded empty infinite loops from single-line form to brace bodies, e.g. `while (true) { }` style in camera.c.
Adjusted cast spacing in several examples from forms like `(u8*)&value` to `(u8*) &value`, `(s32)fn` to `(s32) fn`, and function-pointer casts like `(s32(*)(Vec3*)) cb`.
Removed extra blank lines and normalized comment spacing, e.g. `cm_803BCCA0; // Force load`.
Applied formatting to both C implementation files and headers, including forward/type headers and static headers.
smart_moves: Isolated formatting as its own PR, making it easier to review as mechanical cleanup rather than mixing style churn with semantic decomp changes.
Used clang-format consistently across a broad source tree instead of hand-formatting individual files, reducing subjective style drift.
Merged quickly with no recorded review discussion, consistent with a mechanical formatting pass where the title and diff make the intent obvious.
By normalizing existing code before more decomp work, future functional PRs can have smaller diffs that are less cluttered by unrelated whitespace and wrapping changes.
decomp_lessons: For large decomp repositories, run formatter-only changes separately from matching or symbol-renaming work; otherwise reviewers cannot easily distinguish semantic edits from style churn.
Do not infer algorithmic or matching changes from this PR: the slice evidence shows mechanical clang-format output, not behavior changes.

Formatting can touch sensitive matching code without intending to affect generated code; future authors should still verify builds/matches after formatter sweeps because C source layout can interact with decomp-permuter-era constructs and macro-heavy code.
Expect clang-format to rewrap long expressions in camera, fighter, item, stage, menu, and baselib files; future patches should follow the formatted baseline to avoid needless diff noise.
Header formatting is part of source hygiene too: forward declarations, type headers, static headers, and function prototypes were included in the formatting pass.
naming_conventions: No new symbol naming convention was introduced by the evidence in this slice.
Existing subsystem prefixes remained unchanged, including `Camera_`, `Stage_`, `ft`, `gm`, `mn`, `it`, `lb`, `gr`, and `HSD_` names.
Existing decomp-style anonymous or address-derived names such as `cm_80452C68`, `cm_803BCCA0`, and `fn_8002F908` were preserved; this PR did not rename them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic was explicitly documented in the PR text or review comments.

The diff preserves visible decomp/permuter notes such as `/// @remarks permuter jank` and `// Force load`; clang-format changed spacing/wrapping around them but did not replace the underlying matching workaround.
Formatter changes around casts, long expressions, and inline functions should be treated as mechanical unless a future build/match check indicates otherwise.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice.
There is no recorded reviewer rationale or requested change to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mention clang-format regressions or matching fallout after PR 2066.
Search for repository clang-format configuration changes near January 2026.
Search future review comments for requests to keep formatter-only changes separate from semantic changes.
Compare post-PR formatting conventions for casts, inline functions, and long ternary expressions in src/melee/cm/camera.c.
